title: Custom Functions
description: Extend agent capabilities with custom functions and integrations

sections.title: Functions & Skills
sections.subtitle: Pre-built capabilities for agents to interact with external services
sections.description: Functions are pre-built capabilities that enable agents to perform specific tasks and integrate with external services. AgentLog includes system functions for core operations and provider functions for service integrations.

sections.title: What are Functions?
sections.content:
Functions extend agent capabilities by providing:

• **GitHub Operations**: Create issues, PRs, read code, search repositories, manage branches
• **Slack Communication**: Send messages, manage channels, read conversations, add reactions
• **Memory Management**: Agent and team memory operations for persistent context
• **Google Drive Integration**: Access files, search documents, manage content
• **Weather Services**: Get current weather data for any location

items.title: JSON Schema Validation
items.description: Define clear input/output schemas that ensure reliable function execution and data integrity

items.title: Flexible Implementation
items.description: Support for multiple programming languages and execution environments to fit your needs

items.title: Error Handling
items.description: Built-in error handling and validation to ensure robust agent execution even when functions fail

items.title: Performance Monitoring
items.description: Track function performance, execution times, and success rates across all agent runs

steps.title: Access Functions
steps.description: Navigate to the Functions section to view and manage custom functions

steps.title: Create New Function
steps.description: Click 'New Function' to start building your custom integration
steps.details: You'll need to define the function name, description, and implementation details

steps.title: Define Schema
steps.description: Specify input parameters and expected output format using JSON schema
steps.details: Clear schemas help agents understand how to use your function correctly

steps.title: Implement Logic
steps.description: Write the function code that processes inputs and returns results
steps.details: Use your preferred programming language and include proper error handling

steps.title: Test Function
steps.description: Test your function with sample inputs to ensure it works correctly
steps.details: Validate that inputs are processed correctly and outputs match expected schema

steps.title: Attach to Agents
steps.description: Make your function available to agents by attaching it to their configuration

items.title: GitHub Create Issue
items.description: Create new issues in GitHub repositories with labels and assignees
items.usecase: Bug tracking, feature requests, automated issue creation from agent analysis

items.title: Slack Send Message
items.description: Send messages to Slack channels or users with rich formatting
items.usecase: Team notifications, status updates, automated reporting to Slack channels

items.title: Agent Memory Write
items.description: Store information in agent's persistent memory for future reference
items.usecase: Remembering user preferences, storing context across conversations, learning from interactions

items.title: Get Current Weather
items.description: Retrieve current weather conditions for any location
items.usecase: Weather-based decision making, location-aware responses, environmental context

sections.title: Function Schema Structure
Input Validation
items: **Schema Compliance**: Validate all inputs against defined schema
**Type Checking**: Ensure data types match expectations
**Required Fields**: Check that all required parameters are provided
**Value Ranges**: Validate numeric ranges and string lengths

sections.title: Error Handling
items: **Status Codes**: Use appropriate HTTP status codes for web functions
**Logging**: Log errors for debugging and monitoring
**Fallback Logic**: Provide default behaviors when possible

sections.title: Performance
items: **Resource Management**: Clean up connections and resources
**Async Operations**: Use asynchronous processing for I/O operations

sections.title: Best Practices
tips: 📝 **Clear Documentation**: Provide detailed descriptions for function purpose and usage
🔒 **Secure Credentials**: Never hardcode API keys or sensitive information in functions
⚡ **Optimize Performance**: Keep functions fast and efficient for better agent response times
🧪 **Thorough Testing**: Test functions with various input scenarios and edge cases
📊 **Monitor Usage**: Track function performance and error rates across executions
🔄 **Version Control**: Maintain function versions for rollback and change tracking
🎯 **Single Responsibility**: Design functions to do one thing well rather than multiple tasks

solutions: Optimize function code for better performance
Increase timeout settings if processing takes longer
Use asynchronous operations for I/O bound tasks
Break complex operations into smaller, faster functions
Verify function schema matches actual implementation
Check that all required parameters are properly defined
Test with sample inputs to validate schema correctness
Review parameter types and format requirements
Verify API credentials and permissions are correct
Check API endpoint URLs and request formats
Handle rate limiting and retry failed requests
Implement proper error handling for API responses
Implement proper error handling and logging
Test functions with edge cases and invalid inputs
Use consistent data formats for inputs and outputs
